introSection: Big Bad John is a steel hybrid roller coaster located at Magic Springs & Crystal Falls in Hot Springs, Arkansas, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The coaster was one of two similar coasters placed side-by-side that together formed the River King Mine Train, an opening year attraction at Six Flags Over Mid-America. One track opened with the park on June 5, 1971 while the second opened on August 10, bringing the reported capacity to 5,000 riders per hour.

It was renamed River King Run-Away Mine Train the following year.

For the 1984 season, Six Flags restored the original River King Mine Train name.

After the 1987 season, this coaster closed and was left standing but not operating in 1988, before being sold and relocated to Dollywood. The second track continues to operate at the park, now Six Flags St. Louis.

It operated from 1989 to 1998 as the Thunder Express. It was then moved to Magic Springs & Crystal Falls, where it reopened in 2002 as the Big Bad John.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Black track with unpainted wooden supports.

fullText:
Big Bad John is a steel hybrid roller coaster located at Magic Springs & Crystal Falls in Hot Springs, Arkansas, USA.

The coaster was one of two similar coasters placed side-by-side that together formed the River King Mine Train, an opening year attraction at Six Flags Over Mid-America. One track opened with the park on June 5, 1971 while the second opened on August 10, bringing the reported capacity to 5,000 riders per hour.

It was renamed River King Run-Away Mine Train the following year.

For the 1984 season, Six Flags restored the original River King Mine Train name.

After the 1987 season, this coaster closed and was left standing but not operating in 1988, before being sold and relocated to Dollywood. The second track continues to operate at the park, now Six Flags St. Louis.

It operated from 1989 to 1998 as the Thunder Express. It was then moved to Magic Springs & Crystal Falls, where it reopened in 2002 as the Big Bad John.



Black track with unpainted wooden supports.
